summary: Design + adversarially review the surgical (worker-state-flag-gated) A10-v2 fix for the F44 lockdown-reopen wedge

findings: Lockdown poll entered before 200ms (PMU n=4); naive flag safe
Fast-fail exits earlier at WPR2-up gate, never reaching the poll
Arm at poll-entry not lock-acquire; read nv.c:1902 before C2 nv.c:1914
openQuestions: Set flag before first cond eval; clear at poll-exit and open entry

codesites.summary: A10-v2 surgical worker-in-lockdown-poll flag: code-site map with exact file:line for set/clear/read/gate, HAL routing proof, crux-race resolution, A7 verdict, and memory ordering.
findings: POLL SITE the 5090 runs: kernel_gsp_gh100.c:1050 gpuTimeoutCondWait(pGpu,_kgspLockdownReleasedOrFmcError,pKernelGsp,NULL) in kgspBootstrap_GH100 (opens gh100.c:935). Loop ENTRY=1050, EXIT=1050/1051 boundary. Cond fn at gh100.c:499 reads via kflcnRegRead_HAL which route osDevReadReg032 -> osIsGpuBusDead(os.c:1884) -> os_pci_is_disconnected; C2 short-circuits these.

HAL ROUTING: g_kernel_gsp_nvoc.c:786-805 kgspBootstrap halsel; GB202 falls into the ELSE branch -> kgspBootstrap_GH100 (line 803). blackwell/*.c has NO kgspBootstrap override (gb100.c only kgspResetHw_GB100:83, unrelated reset path). Edit gh100.c ONLY; gb100.c is off-path.

STORAGE: NvU32 tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll on nv_state_t, placed near is_external_gpu (line 556) in BOTH kernel-open/common/inc/nv.h and src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/include/nv.h (both structs close at 657). RM reaches it via NV_GET_NV_STATE(pGpu) (macro unix nv.h:862). Proven precedent: os.c cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic writes per-GPU gpu_lost_detector_logged (nv.h:655) this exact way. WRITE_ONCE set/clear, READ_ONCE read; atomic_t overkill (single writer thread, monotone within an open).

SET (worker/RM): gh100.c immediately before 1050: nv=NV_GET_NV_STATE(pGpu); if(nv) WRITE_ONCE(nv->tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll, NV_TRUE). CLEAR: immediately after 1050 before if(status) at 1051: same with NV_FALSE. pGpu is first formal of kgspBootstrap_GH100 (937), in scope. Direct field write = 2 set + 2 clear deep-RM lines, no new os-interface symbol; use an os_* helper-pair (clone os_pci_set/is_disconnected, os-interface.h:117, os-pci.c:165) ONLY if NV_GET_NV_STATE is not visible from this TU.

GATE (foreground/kernel-open): nv.c:1914, the A6 OPEN timeout else-branch. Wrap: if(READ_ONCE(nv->tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll)) os_pci_set_disconnected(nv->handle). Fast-fail flag=FALSE -> C2 suppressed (fixes the permanent-dead-bus regression); lockdown flag=TRUE -> C2 fires as in A10-v1. C5 sink rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state at nv.c:1919 already uses COND_ACQUIRE (osapi.c:1913) and cannot deadlock; only the C2 marker needs gating.

A7 SHUTDOWN C2 (nv.c:2322): KEEP UNCONDITIONAL. The teardown path has no two-substrate split - a genuine A7 timeout means the teardown RPC truly hung, with no ~205ms WPR2-fast-fail twin. The shutdown worker GSP stall (~600ms unload RPC) is NOT the kgspBootstrap lockdown poll, so tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll reads FALSE there; gating A7 would wrongly suppress C2 when needed. A8 teardown variant (nv.c:2342) already avoids stranding state. The surgical gate is OPEN-PATH ONLY.

CRUX RACE RESOLVED. r2 dmesg (r2-adversarial-bound-20260601T052204Z/i1-cycle2-dmesg.txt): open scheduled @5322.943107, timed out after 200ms @5323.148672 (205.6ms), RmInitAdapter failed 0x62:0x40:2131 @5323.150986 (~208ms). On the WPR2-fast-fail substrate the worker fails RmInitAdapter at ~205-208ms WITHOUT entering the multi-second lockdown poll, so the flag is never set and C2 is correctly suppressed. On the lockdown substrate (wedge-2026-06-02 doc lines 33-37) the foreground DEADLOCKED in rm_cleanup's blocking acquire AT the 200ms mark, proving the worker was inside kgspBootstrap holding the API lock by 200ms (FSP boot + target-mask-release steps before line 1050 are sub-100ms). So a poll-entry-set flag is reliably visible at the 200ms read on the substrate that needs it.

RESIDUAL RACE + CLOSER: poll-ENTRY time is INFERRED from the deadlock-at-200ms, not directly instrumented, so a ~195-200ms entry window remains. Close it WITHOUT reintroducing the fast-fail regression by arming earlier: set the flag at kgspBootstrap_GH100 entry (gh100.c:941, clear at 1051) so the fast-fail worker sets-then-clears on its own quick return (foreground sees FALSE) while the lockdown worker sets-and-stalls-before-clear; AND/OR add a bounded ~20-50ms grace re-wait (sized to the ~5-8ms overshoot margin) in the nv.c else-branch that re-checks completion then re-reads the flag. The fast-fail twin has complete()'d -EIO by ~208ms, so the grace re-wait sees worker-done there and flag=TRUE+not-done on the lockdown twin - clean separation.

MEMORY ORDERING: writer = system_long_wq kthread (nv_f40b_open_worker -> nv_open_device_for_nvlfp -> kgspBootstrap_GH100 poll); reader = opener thread in nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded after wait_for_completion_timeout returns 0. Use WRITE_ONCE on set/clear and READ_ONCE on the read to prevent tearing/hoisting; sufficient because the flag is single-writer, monotone-within-open, and C2 is idempotent. Do NOT rely on the completion release-acquire edge: on the return-0 timeout path it has not fired. The CLEAR is ordered after the poll by placing WRITE_ONCE(false) after gh100.c:1050 (gpuTimeoutCondWait return is a compiler barrier). On this x86 host plain WRITE_ONCE/READ_ONCE suffices; if the grace-rewait closer is added, add smp_wmb() after the worker set and smp_rmb() before the foreground read for weakly-ordered arches. Also add a defensive WRITE_ONCE(false) at nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded entry (before queue_work) so a prior open's residual TRUE cannot leak into a later fast-fail open's timeout read.
openQuestions: Poll-entry time is inferred from deadlock-at-200ms, not instrumented; one passive printf at gh100.c:1050 entry decides set-at-1050 vs the safer set-at-941.
Is NV_GET_NV_STATE visible from the gh100.c TU? If not, use an os_* helper-pair rather than pulling unix nv.h into the GSP TU.
Clear the flag on normal worker completion and at open entry (defensive WRITE_ONCE false at nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded entry) to avoid stale TRUE across re-opens.
If the grace-rewait closer is adopted, validate the ~20-50ms window on WPR2-fast-fail (n>=3, never re-arms C2) and on one lockdown fire (still arms C2).

v1delta.summary: A10-v1 (commit d2a4e514 on branch a10-f40b-lockfree-sink, NOT pushed) makes exactly TWO source changes plus reuses a pre-existing R0 flush_work guard. (C2): inserts os_pci_set_disconnected(nv->handle) UNCONDITIONALLY at the top of BOTH F40b timeout branches — the A6 OPEN branch (nv.c:1914) and the A7 SHUTDOWN branch (nv.c:2322). (C1): flips rmapiLockAcquire NONE->COND_ACQUIRE in rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state (osapi.c:1913). The regression is source-confirmed: the A6 OPEN timeout branch is the SAME branch the common WPR2-already-up fast-fail takes (forensic delta 205-210ms, just over the 200ms budget), and C2 marks the bus pci_channel_io_perm_failure PERMANENTLY (a sink with no transition out, os-pci.c:172-183), stranding the chip dead-bus until module reload. v2 must wrap the C2 line at nv.c:1914 in a worker-state flag-gate so it fires ONLY on the genuine lockdown-poll substrate. CRUX RACE RESOLVED IN FAVOR OF A POLL-ENTRY/LOCK-ACQUIRE FLAG: the two substrates diverge at a hard source boundary — the fast-fail returns at kernel_gsp.c:4763-4768 BEFORE kgspBootstrap_GH100 runs, so its worker NEVER enters the lockdown poll and NEVER sets the flag; the lockdown substrate passes that check and enters gpuTimeoutCondWait at gh100.c:1050 for seconds. Forensics prove the foreground deadlocked in rm_cleanup at the 200ms mark on the lockdown substrate, proving the worker held the API lock (was in init) by 200ms. A residual micro-race (poll entered AT ~200ms) is cheaply closeable.
findings: C2 EXACT INSERTION, A6 OPEN branch (the line v2 must gate): kernel-open/nvidia/nv.c:1914 `os_pci_set_disconnected(nv->handle);` sits inside the else/timeout branch of nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded (the `jiffies_left > 0` if at nv.c:1889; else opens nv.c:1895). Branch order: timeout nv_printf 1897-1900 -> A10 comment 1902-1913 -> C2 os_pci_set_disconnected(nv->handle) @1914 -> C5 comment 1916-1918 -> rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state(sp,nv,NV_GPU_LOST_DETECTOR_AER_FATAL) @1919 -> nv_tb_egpu_f40b_fired() @1923 (A8) -> R0 UAF comment 1925-1956 -> flush_work(&w->work) @1957 -> rc=-EIO @1959. v2 wraps ONLY line 1914 (and its 1902-1913 comment) in `if (<worker-in-lockdown-poll flag set>) { ... }`. Leave C5/A8/flush_work/rc=-EIO unchanged.

C2 EXACT INSERTION, A7 SHUTDOWN branch: kernel-open/nvidia/nv.c:2322 `os_pci_set_disconnected(nv->handle);` inside nv_f40b_shutdown_bounded's timeout else-branch (else opens ~2308). Same order: timeout msg 2310-2314 -> A10 comment 2316-2321 -> C2 @2322 -> C5 rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state @2327 -> nv_tb_egpu_f40b_fired_teardown() @2342 (A8 teardown) -> SH-3 UAF comment 2344-2365 -> flush_work(&w->work) @2366. SCOPE NOTE: the regression is OPEN-path-only (the WPR2-fast-fail re-open hits the A6 branch). A7's budget is 1200-2000ms (NVreg_TbEgpuShutdownTimeoutMs) and its dominant fire is benign teardown; recommend gating A7 C2 with the SAME flag for symmetry, but A7 C2 is NOT implicated in the regression.

C1 (osapi.c) STAYS UNCHANGED IN v2 — confirmed. One-line diff at src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/src/osapi.c:1913 `rmapiLockAcquire(API_LOCK_FLAGS_COND_ACQUIRE, RM_LOCK_MODULES_DESTROY)` (was API_LOCK_FLAGS_NONE), inside rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state (osapi.c:1889). The else/deferring branch (osapi.c:1923-1933) is the now-reachable best-effort path. C1 is INDEPENDENT of the fast-fail regression and is load-bearing for the AER error_detected callsite (nv-pci.c) AND the A2 QWD which also call rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state — keep it so those callers do not block behind a worker-held lock. C1 does NOT permanently sink the bus by itself: it only reaches cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic (os.c:1921) when the lock is FREE, and that is idempotent + fires only if PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST is already set — benign on a transient fast-fail. The PERMANENT dead-bus marker comes ONLY from C2's direct os_pci_set_disconnected. NO interaction issue with gating C2.

R0 UAF flush_work GUARD MUST STAY and is UNAFFECTED by gating C2. flush_work(&w->work) at nv.c:1957 blocks until the worker leaves nv_open_device_for_nvlfp (protects nvlfp/sp; the refcount-2 protocol protects only the work struct). It is AFTER the C2 line, not inside the gate. On FAST-FAIL with C2 gated OFF: the worker already returned -EIO at ~205-210ms (RmInitAdapter-failed logs at 5323.150986, ~2ms after the 5323.148672 timeout), so flush_work joins instantly (work already complete) — NO blocking, NO sink, error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal => chip RECOVERABLE (matches deployed self-heal: r2 logged rc=0 re-opens after fast-fails). Branch still returns rc=-EIO @1959. NO other path strands the chip with C2 gated off: C1 is no-op-on-recoverable; C5 rm_cleanup's only permanent action (cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic) short-circuits when the GPU is healthy. The ONLY permanent sinker on this branch is the C2 line — gating it is sufficient and complete.

CRUX RACE — RESOLVED; a poll-entry/lock-acquire flag is reliable, with one cheap race-closer recommended. HARD SOURCE BOUNDARY: the WPR2-fast-fail returns NV_ERR_INVALID_STATE at src/nvidia/src/kernel/gpu/gsp/kernel_gsp.c:4763-4768 ('Fail early if WPR2 is up') inside _kgspBootGspRm, BEFORE kgspBootstrap_GH100 — so the fast-fail worker provably NEVER reaches the lockdown poll at gh100.c:1050 and NEVER sets a poll-entry flag (zero false-positive on the common path => zero regression by construction). The LOCKDOWN substrate passes line 4763, enters kgspBootstrap_GH100, and BLOCKS at gpuTimeoutCondWait(_kgspLockdownReleasedOrFmcError) gh100.c:1050 for the full gpuTimeout (~4s gfx/~30s compute). TIMING PROOF worker is in init by 200ms on the lockdown substrate: the live wedge RCA shows the foreground DEADLOCKED on rm_cleanup's BLOCKING rmapiLockAcquire at the 200ms mark — which can ONLY block if the worker already HELD the RM API write-lock (past lock-acquire, inside RmInitAdapter). RESIDUAL MICRO-RACE: a flag set exactly AT gh100.c:1050 is visible only if the worker reaches 1050 before the 200ms read; the lock is acquired EARLIER in init than 1050, so a sub-window exists where worker holds lock but has not reached 1050. RACE-CLOSER (pick one): (a) ARM THE FLAG AT API-LOCK-ACQUIRE / kgspBootstrap-entry (strictly before the contended lock — deterministic); OR (b) bounded grace re-wait (~50ms wait_for_completion_timeout re-check) in the nv.c timeout branch before reading the flag (the fast-fail worker will have RETURNED — flag stays unset; the lockdown worker will have set it). Neither reintroduces the fast-fail regression because the fast-fail worker has terminated and cannot set the flag.

FLAG HOME — reachable from BOTH sites, sovereignty-minimal. Set/clear at the RM poll site (deep RM: gh100.c near 1050 or at kgspBootstrap entry); read at the kernel-open foreground (nv.c:1914 gate). NV_GET_NV_STATE(pGpu) at nv.h:862 maps OBJGPU->nv_state_t (pOsGpuInfo); the foreground already holds the same nv. RECOMMENDED HOME: an os-interface helper pair (e.g. os_tb_egpu_set_in_lockdown_poll / os_tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll) declared in kernel-open/common/inc/os-interface.h next to os_pci_is_disconnected (@116-117) and implemented in the nv-tb-egpu-* surface, backed by a NvBool/atomic field added to nv_state_t (nv.h, alongside is_external_gpu @556). RM-side edit stays ONE call (set on entry to gpuTimeoutCondWait at gh100.c:1050, clear on its return), reachable via the existing os-interface boundary — no new deep-RM machinery. Alternative (flag on worker struct nv_f40b_open_work nv.c:1826) is NOT reachable from the RM poll site, so it would route through nv_state_t anyway; prefer the nv_state_t + os-interface route.

FILES + HUNKS v2 WILL TOUCH (delta on top of a10): (1) kernel-open/nvidia/nv.c — wrap line 1914 (C2 open) in the flag-gate; optionally wrap line 2322 (C2 shutdown) for symmetry [recommend yes]; ~6-12 lines. (2) RM poll site src/nvidia/src/kernel/gpu/gsp/arch/hopper/kernel_gsp_gh100.c — set flag before gpuTimeoutCondWait @1050, clear after (@~1051/1066/1111); justified deep-RM edit ~3-4 lines, the MINIMAL site since GB202 binds kgspBootstrap__ to kgspBootstrap_GH100 per generated/g_kernel_gsp_nvoc.c:803. (3) kernel-open/common/inc/os-interface.h AND src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/include/os-interface.h (BOTH mirror @116-117) — add the os_tb_egpu_* prototype pair. (4) nv-tb-egpu-* (recommend nv-tb-egpu-recover.c/.h or a small new TU) — implement the helper. (5) kernel-open/common/inc/nv.h — add NvBool/atomic field to nv_state_t near @556 (also mirror to src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/include/nv.h if it diverges). (6) if race-closer (b) chosen, add the bounded grace re-wait in nv.c timeout branch (no extra file). C1 (osapi.c:1913) and the R0 flush_work (nv.c:1957) are NOT touched.

COMMIT/VERSION DISPOSITION: branch a10-f40b-lockfree-sink HEAD is d2a4e514 (NOT pushed). RECOMMEND STACK A NEW v2 COMMIT on top of d2a4e514 (do NOT amend) — A10-v1's unconditional C2 is itself the live-validated containment for the lockdown wedge, and the substrate-divergence story is cleaner as v1(contain)->v2(de-regress) two commits for the eventual upstream/catalog narrative (matches per-mechanism catalog discipline). version.mk working-tree already shows NVIDIA_VERSION = 595.71.05-apnex.26 (M in git status; NVIDIA_NVID_VERSION = 595.71.05 unchanged); the apnex.25->apnex.26 bump is staged for this fix; v2 lands under apnex.26. GOTCHA (task #294): the apnex.26 bump needs a matching /lib/firmware/nvidia/595.71.05 symlink at deploy (NVIDIA_NVID_VERSION unchanged so the firmware path is unchanged here, but verify the build's firmware symlink resolves before reboot).
openQuestions: Race-closer choice: (a) arm the flag at API-lock-acquire / kgspBootstrap-entry (strictly-before the contended lock — eliminates the window deterministically but sets the flag slightly broader than 'in lockdown poll') vs (b) bounded grace re-wait (~50ms) in the nv.c timeout branch before reading the flag (keeps flag semantics narrowly poll-entry-scoped, adds up to ~50ms only on the genuine-wedge path). Recommend (a) for determinism; (b) if you want the flag to literally mean 'in the lockdown poll'. Needs the user's call.

Should the A7 SHUTDOWN-path C2 (nv.c:2322) ALSO be gated, or left unconditional? The regression is OPEN-path-only; the shutdown branch's dominant fire is benign teardown and its budget is 1200-2000ms. Gating it symmetrically is cheap and consistent; leaving it unconditional is also defensible (a shutdown timeout never re-couples to a fast-fail re-open). Lean: gate both for symmetry.

Flag storage type: plain NvBool in nv_state_t (single-writer worker / single-reader foreground, separated by the wait_for_completion_timeout barrier) vs atomic_t / WRITE_ONCE+READ_ONCE for explicit cross-CPU visibility (mirroring os-pci.c's WRITE_ONCE pattern @183). Recommend WRITE_ONCE/READ_ONCE for the worker/foreground CPU boundary.

nv_state_t is mirrored in TWO trees (kernel-open/common/inc/nv.h and src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/include/nv.h) and os-interface.h exists in both (@116-117): confirm whether the build uses one canonical copy or both must be edited in lockstep, and place the new field/prototype accordingly.

design.summary: A10-v2 SURGICAL design: gate the C2 dead-bus marker (os_pci_set_disconnected) at nv.c:1914 on a worker-set "in lockdown busy-poll" flag stored on nv_state_t, set/cleared by the RM GSP-bootstrap poll site in kernel_gsp_gh100.c and read by the kernel-open foreground A6 timeout branch. The two failure substrates diverge at a HARD SOURCE BOUNDARY: the WPR2-up fast-fail returns NV_ERR_INVALID_STATE at kernel_gsp.c:4768 inside _kgspBootGspRm, BEFORE the kgspBootstrap_HAL call at kernel_gsp.c:4798, so the fast-fail worker provably never enters kgspBootstrap_GH100 and never sets the flag -> C2 is suppressed on the common path (fixing the permanent-dead-bus regression and restoring self-heal). The lockdown substrate passes line 4768, enters kgspBootstrap_GH100, and stalls at gpuTimeoutCondWait(_kgspLockdownReleasedOrFmcError) at gh100.c:1050 holding the RM API write-lock -> flag is set -> C2 fires as in A10-v1, short-circuiting the worker's MMIO reads to dead-bus so the lockdown cond returns true, worker self-terminates, flush_work joins, -EIO returned, no deadlock. The crux race is closed by arming the flag at kgspBootstrap_GH100 ENTRY (gh100.c:941) rather than at poll-entry, so even a poll entered at ~200ms is already flagged; clear at poll-exit (after gh100.c:1050). C1 (osapi.c:1913 COND_ACQUIRE), the R0 flush_work UAF guard (nv.c:1957), and A8 (nv.c:1923) are untouched. A7 shutdown-path C2 (nv.c:2322) stays UNCONDITIONAL — its substrate has no fast-fail twin and tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll reads FALSE there anyway, so gating it would wrongly suppress C2 on a genuine teardown hang.
findings: FLAG FIELD: add `NvU32 tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll;` to nv_state_t immediately after `is_external_gpu` (nv.h:556) in BOTH mirrored trees kernel-open/common/inc/nv.h:556 and src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/include/nv.h (same struct, edit in lockstep — the build compiles both surfaces). Use NvU32 not NvBool for unambiguous WRITE_ONCE/READ_ONCE word semantics. Precedent for per-GPU RM-side writes to nv_state_t is gpu_lost_detector_logged, written via NV_GET_NV_STATE(pGpu) from os.c — same mechanism.

SET/CLEAR (deep-RM, kernel_gsp_gh100.c — the MINIMAL and ONLY poll TU; GB202 binds kgspBootstrap__ -> kgspBootstrap_GH100 per generated/g_kernel_gsp_nvoc.c:803; blackwell/kernel_gsp_gb100.c has NO kgspBootstrap override so it is OFF-path, do not touch it). ARM AT BOOTSTRAP ENTRY, not poll-entry, to close the race: at gh100.c:941 (top of kgspBootstrap_GH100 body, pGpu in scope as first formal) insert `{ nv_state_t *nv = NV_GET_NV_STATE(pGpu); if (nv) WRITE_ONCE(nv->tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll, NV_TRUE); }`. CLEAR after the poll returns: immediately after gh100.c:1050 (before the `if (status)` at 1051) insert the symmetric WRITE_ONCE(..., NV_FALSE). Also CLEAR on every early `return status` between 941 and 1050 (the kfspSendBootCommands/target-mask/SPDM/initRPC failure returns at ~971/999/1014/1029/1044) so a pre-poll bootstrap failure does not leave the flag stuck TRUE — simplest is a single `goto out_clear;`-style epilogue, or clear-then-return at each. NOTE: a pre-poll bootstrap failure is NOT the lockdown substrate, so leaving the flag TRUE there would arm C2 incorrectly on a recoverable failure — the clear-on-all-exits is REQUIRED, not optional.

FOREGROUND GATE (kernel-open, nv.c:1914): wrap ONLY the C2 line (and its 1902-1913 comment) in `if (READ_ONCE(nv->tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll)) { os_pci_set_disconnected(nv->handle); }`. Leave C5 rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state @1919, A8 nv_tb_egpu_f40b_fired() @1923, the R0 flush_work @1957, and rc=-EIO @1959 UNCHANGED and OUTSIDE the gate. On fast-fail: flag FALSE -> C2 skipped -> error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal -> chip recoverable; the worker already returned -EIO at ~205-210ms so flush_work joins instantly. On lockdown: flag TRUE -> C2 fires -> worker self-terminates -> flush_work joins fast.

RACE ARGUMENT (resolved): the only way the gate is wrong is a false-NEGATIVE on the lockdown substrate (flag not yet TRUE at the 200ms foreground read while the worker IS stuck). Arming at kgspBootstrap_GH100 ENTRY (gh100.c:941) eliminates this: the worker reaches 941 strictly BEFORE it can acquire the RM API write-lock and stall at the poll (FSP boot + target-mask-release + initRPCs are all AFTER 941 but BEFORE 1050). Forensics prove the foreground DEADLOCKED in rm_cleanup's blocking acquire AT the 200ms mark on the lockdown substrate (wedge-2026-06-02 doc:33-37), which can only happen if the worker already HELD the API write-lock by 200ms — and the lock is acquired AFTER entry 941. Therefore by 200ms the worker is provably past 941, so the flag is provably TRUE. No residual race remains; the ~195-200ms poll-entry uncertainty from the CODE-SITE finding is moot because the arm point is at entry, not poll-entry. False-POSITIVE is impossible by the hard boundary: the fast-fail worker returns at kernel_gsp.c:4768 before kgspBootstrap_HAL at 4798, so it never reaches gh100.c:941.

WHY NO grace-rewait is needed: with the entry-arm, the entry-vs-200ms ordering is settled by the deadlock proof, so the optional bounded grace re-wait (closer (b) from the reviewers) is NOT required and is omitted to keep the change minimal and avoid adding up to ~50ms latency on the genuine-wedge path. Keep it documented as the fallback if instrumentation ever shows the lock can be acquired after 200ms (it cannot, per the deadlock evidence).

MEMORY ORDERING: writer = system_long_wq kthread (nv_f40b_open_worker -> nv_open_device_for_nvlfp -> ... -> kgspBootstrap_GH100); reader = opener thread after wait_for_completion_timeout returns 0 (the return-0 path has NOT taken the completion release-acquire edge, so do not rely on it). Use WRITE_ONCE on set/clear and READ_ONCE on the gate read — sufficient on this x86 host (single writer, monotone-within-open, C2 idempotent). Add a DEFENSIVE WRITE_ONCE(nv->tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll, NV_FALSE) at nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded entry (nv.c, before queue_work at nv.c:1884) so a prior open's residual TRUE cannot leak into a later fast-fail open's timeout read. If the grace-rewait fallback is ever adopted, add smp_wmb() after the worker set and smp_rmb() before the foreground read for weakly-ordered arches.

A7 SHUTDOWN-PATH C2 (nv.c:2322): KEEP UNCONDITIONAL — do NOT gate. The teardown path has no two-substrate split; a genuine A7 timeout means the unload RPC truly hung (the ~600ms GSP unload is normal and well under the 1200-2000ms A7 budget, so A7 only fires on a real hang). The shutdown worker is NOT in kgspBootstrap's lockdown poll, so tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll reads FALSE there; gating A7 with this flag would WRONGLY suppress C2 exactly when teardown needs containment. The regression is OPEN-path-only. This overrides the 'gate both for symmetry' lean in two reviewer findings — symmetry is wrong here because the flag semantics do not hold on the shutdown substrate.

C1, R0 flush_work, A8 ALL INTACT: C1 (osapi.c:1913 NONE->COND_ACQUIRE) is unchanged and load-bearing for the AER error_detected (nv-pci.c) and A2 QWD callers of rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state; it does not permanently sink the bus (only reaches cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic when lock is free + GPU already lost — benign on transient fast-fail). The R0 flush_work UAF guard (nv.c:1957) stays AFTER and OUTSIDE the gate — mandatory to prevent worker UAF of freed nvlfp/sp; with C2 gated off on fast-fail the worker has already returned so flush_work joins instantly. A8 nv_tb_egpu_f40b_fired() (nv.c:1923) stays unconditional.

FAST-FAIL SELF-HEAL (restored): flag FALSE -> C2 skipped -> os_pci stays pci_channel_io_normal -> C5 rm_cleanup is a no-op-on-healthy -> chip remains recoverable; deployed self-heal resumes (WPR2 clears via close path, subsequent opens rc=0; r2 logged 34 such successes). Matches the deployed (pre-A10) behavior on this substrate. LOCKDOWN CONTAINMENT (preserved): flag TRUE -> C2 marks bus dead -> worker's osDevReadReg032 -> osIsGpuBusDead (os.c:1884) -> os_pci_is_disconnected short-circuits -> _kgspLockdownReleasedOrFmcError returns NV_TRUE -> worker self-terminates in one poll iteration -> RM API write-lock released -> flush_work joins -> -EIO. No lock-inversion deadlock, host stays alive, chip dead-bus until module reload (acceptable for the genuine-wedge path).

RESIDUAL DISCREPANCY TO STRESS-TEST (does NOT block the design but must be checked at build/validate): the in-tree A10-v1 comment at nv.c:1941-1956 states the C5 rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state takes a BLOCKING acquire and re-couples the foreground to the worker for up to gpuTimeout on the lockdown path — but the DELTA finding asserts C1 already flipped that very acquire to COND_ACQUIRE at osapi.c:1913. If C1 is in effect, C5 cannot block and the ordering 'C2 must precede C5' is belt-and-suspenders (C2 still wanted first so the worker fails-fast its MMIO before C5 even tries). If the in-tree comment is stale, update it in v2. Either way C2-before-C5 ordering is correct and preserved; this only affects the comment's accuracy and the worst-case latency claim. Verify by reading osapi.c:1913 on the a10 branch HEAD (d2a4e514) at build time.

COMMIT DISPOSITION: stack a NEW v2 commit on top of d2a4e514 on branch a10-f40b-lockfree-sink (do NOT amend) — v1(contain)->v2(de-regress) is the cleaner two-commit narrative for catalog/upstream. version.mk working tree already at 595.71.05-apnex.26; v2 lands under apnex.26. Task #294 firmware-symlink gotcha: NVIDIA_NVID_VERSION unchanged (595.71.05) so the /lib/firmware/nvidia/595.71.05 path is unchanged, but verify the build's firmware symlink resolves before reboot.
openQuestions: Confirm NV_GET_NV_STATE(pGpu) is visible from the kernel_gsp_gh100.c TU (it is the deep-RM GSP TU, not the unix os.c TU). If unix nv.h is NOT already included there, prefer adding a tiny os-interface helper pair (os_tb_egpu_set/clear_in_lockdown_poll declared in BOTH os-interface.h mirrors @116-117, implemented in the nv-tb-egpu-* surface) over pulling unix nv.h into the GSP TU — this keeps the deep-RM edit to one helper call at entry + one at poll-exit and stays sovereignty-minimal. Decide at implementation time by checking existing includes in kernel_gsp_gh100.c.

Clear-on-all-pre-poll-exits in kgspBootstrap_GH100 (returns at ~971/999/1014/1029/1044) must be implemented so a pre-poll bootstrap failure (FSP/target-mask/SPDM/initRPC) does not leave the flag TRUE and arm C2 on a recoverable non-lockdown failure. Stress-test: inject a target-mask-release failure and confirm flag is FALSE at the foreground read.

Resolve the C5-blocking discrepancy: is osapi.c:1913 COND_ACQUIRE actually present on a10 HEAD d2a4e514 (DELTA finding) or is the nv.c:1941-1956 comment correct that C5 still blocks on the lockdown path? Read osapi.c:1913 at build time; if COND_ACQUIRE is present, update the now-stale nv.c comment in v2.

Live validation required before flipping apnex.26 to production: (1) WPR2-fast-fail substrate n>=3 — confirm flag reads FALSE, C2 NOT armed, error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal, subsequent open rc=0 (self-heal restored). (2) lockdown substrate at least one fire — confirm flag reads TRUE, C2 armed, worker self-terminates in one poll iteration, flush_work joins, -EIO returned, host stays alive (no deadlock/wedge). (3) A7 teardown timeout (if reproducible) — confirm C2 still fires unconditionally with flag FALSE.

Defensive WRITE_ONCE(false) at nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded entry (before queue_work nv.c:1884) — confirm placement does not race the worker (it runs before queue_work, single-threaded at that point, so safe), and confirm no other open path reaches the A6 timeout branch with a stale TRUE.

critical: RE-WEDGE: The design's load-bearing deadlock proof is built on a FALSE lock model. The design asserts (finding RACE ARGUMENT + finding 4) that 'the worker provably HELD the RM API write-lock by 200ms, the lock is acquired AFTER entry 941, therefore the worker is past 941 and the flag is TRUE.' But GSP init uses RELAXED locking on this host: _kgspShouldRelaxGspInitLocking (kernel_gsp.c:4688) returns NV_TRUE because (a) the registry key NV_REG_STR_RM_RELAXED_GSP_INIT_LOCKING is unset so it takes the DEFAULT 0x2 (nvrm_registry.h:2822) which the function treats as relax=TRUE (4712-4715), and (b) SPDM is OFF on a consumer 5090 (PDB_PROP_SPDM_ENABLED is only set TRUE in conf_compute.c, CC mode). Under relaxed locking _kgspBootGspRm RELEASES the API lock at kernel_gsp.c:4785 BEFORE kgspBootstrap_HAL (4798) and reacquires it only AFTER the poll at 4818-4820. So during the gh100.c:1050 lockdown poll the worker holds ONLY the GPU group lock, NOT the API lock. The API lock is held by the worker ONLY during the pre-bootstrap phase (rm_init_adapter osapi.c:1761 -> RmInitAdapter -> kgspInitRm -> _kgspBootGspRm up to 4785) -- which is BEFORE gh100.c:941, i.e. while the flag is still FALSE. Therefore the foreground 'deadlocking on rmapiLockAcquire' (the entire evidentiary basis for 'flag TRUE by 200ms') can ONLY occur while the worker is PRE-941 (flag FALSE), the exact opposite of what the design concludes. The deadlock proof does not establish flag-TRUE; it actually implies flag-FALSE at the contended-API-lock moment.

RE-WEDGE (timing, independent of the lock-model error): Forensic timing puts the WPR2 fast-fail bail (kernel_gsp.c:4768) at ~205ms after open-scheduled (r2 i1: scheduled 5322.943, RmInitAdapter-failed 5323.151 = +207.9ms; r3 i5 prints _kgspBootGspRm WPR2 msg +44us after the 200ms timeout). On the LOCKDOWN substrate the worker PASSES 4768 (WPR2 clear) and then does STRICTLY MORE work before the poll -- kgspPopulateWprMeta (4772), prepare scrubber (4776), kgspPrepareForBootstrap (4780), rmapiLockRelease (4785), then kgspBootstrap_GH100 entry 941, FSP boot commands (971), target-mask wait (986), SPDM, kgspSendInitRpcs (1040) -- and only THEN reaches the poll at gh100.c:1050. So the lockdown poll-entry (and even bootstrap-entry 941) lands AFTER ~205ms, i.e. AFTER the 200ms foreground timeout has already fired. At the foreground's 200ms READ_ONCE the lockdown worker is very plausibly still PRE-941 -> flag FALSE -> v2 SUPPRESSES C2 on the lockdown substrate -> the worker proceeds into the multi-second gpuTimeout poll while the foreground blocks in flush_work (nv.c:1957) holding ldata_lock, and the AER error_detected / rmmod second contender closes the F44 wedge. v2 re-opens the exact wedge it claims to fix. There is ZERO forensic datapoint of the lockdown poll being entered BEFORE 200ms (every r2/r3/r4 run is the fast-fail twin; the only lockdown event is the 2026-06-02 wedge, which produced no poll-entry timestamp).

The flag is NOT 'monotone-within-open' as the MEMORY ORDERING finding asserts. _kgspBootGspRm runs inside a retry loop (kernel_gsp.c:5077 'while (bRetry && bootAttempts < maxGspBootAttempts)'), so kgspBootstrap_GH100 (and thus the 941-set / post-1050-clear pair) can execute multiple times within a single open. Between/within retries the flag legitimately reads FALSE while the worker is genuinely mid-init and will re-enter the poll. A single foreground READ_ONCE at 200ms can therefore catch a FALSE during a retry's pre-bootstrap window even if an earlier attempt had set it TRUE. The clear-on-poll-exit makes this worse: a foreground read just after a post-1050 clear but before the worker either returns or re-enters bootstrap sees FALSE while the worker is still wedged-capable.
concerns: BUILD BREAK on the naive flag wiring: the design's finding SET/CLEAR proposes inserting 'nv_state_t *nv = NV_GET_NV_STATE(pGpu)' directly at gh100.c:941. NV_GET_NV_STATE is defined only in the unix nv.h (nv.h:862), which kernel_gsp_gh100.c does NOT include (it includes os/os.h, gpu/gsp/kernel_gsp.h, published/hopper/* -- verified), and NO deep-RM GSP TU currently references NV_GET_NV_STATE. The naive insertion will not compile. The os-interface helper-pair (the design's own openQuestion 1) is REQUIRED, not optional -- this should be promoted from open-question to a hard design constraint.

The C2 mechanism itself is mechanically SOUND when the flag IS set (I verified the full chain): the poll cond _kgspLockdownReleasedOrFmcError (gh100.c:499) reads via kflcnRegRead_HAL -> regRead032 -> _regRead -> osDevReadReg032 (gpu_access.c:648), which short-circuits on osIsGpuBusDead -> os_pci_is_disconnected (os.c:1884/2050) returning NV_GPU_BUS_DEAD_VALUE_U32 (0xFFFFFFFF). With mailbox0=0xFFFFFFFF the cond returns NV_TRUE (gh100.c:524 mailbox!=0 with the boot-args-phys-addr guard not matching 0xFFFFFFFF, falling through to line 540's '|| (mailbox0 != 0)'). So the containment works IF AND ONLY IF the flag is reliably TRUE at the foreground read -- which is exactly what the two critical findings show is NOT guaranteed.

Under relaxed locking + C1 (COND_ACQUIRE), the path may be safer than the design's worst-case framing in one respect: cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic (os.c:1921) takes NO GPU group lock and itself calls os_pci_set_disconnected (os.c:1942). So when the worker is IN the poll (API lock free), the foreground's C5 rm_cleanup COND_ACQUIRE succeeds and sets the dead-bus marker anyway -- meaning on the poll-substrate C5 is a SECOND path to C2. This partially undermines the premise that C2-before-C5 is load-bearing, and means the in-tree nv.c:1941-1956 comment claiming C5 takes a BLOCKING acquire and re-couples for gpuTimeout is STALE (C1 already flipped it to COND at osapi.c:1913 -- confirmed on a10 HEAD d2a4e514). The design flags this discrepancy (finding RESIDUAL DISCREPANCY) but does not follow it to its conclusion: if C5-COND already sets C2 whenever the API lock is free, the gating of the separate C2 line at nv.c:1914 has limited additional value on the poll-substrate and ZERO value on the pre-poll-substrate (where both C2 and C5-COND are suppressed/deferred because the worker holds the API lock).

A7 shutdown-path unconditional C2 (nv.c:2322) is the right call to keep, but note its in-tree comment makes the SAME 'worker stalls in a GSP poll holding the RM API lock' claim that the relaxed-locking analysis undercuts for the open path. The shutdown RPC path is different code (not kgspBootstrap) so the lock model may genuinely differ, but this should be re-audited rather than assumed -- the symmetry argument the design uses to justify NOT gating A7 ('flag reads FALSE there') is correct, but the underlying lock-holding claim in the A7 comment deserves the same scrutiny applied here.

No OTHER residual error_state sink was found that would defeat the de-regression intent: with C2 gated off and C5-COND deferring (worker holds API lock on fast-fail... actually fast-fail worker has RETURNED by ~207ms so API lock is free, C5-COND succeeds -> cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic runs -> os_pci_set_disconnected SETS the dead-bus marker on the fast-fail path too!). This is a SEPARATE regression vector the design misses: on the fast-fail substrate the worker returns ~2ms after the 200ms timeout, so by the time the foreground reaches C5 rm_cleanup the API lock is FREE, COND_ACQUIRE SUCCEEDS, and cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic (os.c:1942) calls os_pci_set_disconnected -- permanently marking the bus dead EVEN WITH C2 GATED OFF. Gating only the nv.c:1914 C2 line does NOT restore fast-fail self-heal unless C5's internal os_pci_set_disconnected is ALSO suppressed on the fast-fail substrate. This must be verified: is PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST already set (making cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic a no-op at os.c:1935) on the transient fast-fail, or does C5 sink the bus regardless?
suggestedChanges: Do NOT arm the flag at kgspBootstrap_GH100 entry (gh100.c:941). The timing evidence shows 941 is reached AFTER 200ms on the lockdown substrate, so an entry-arm is still FALSE at the foreground read. Move the arm point EARLIER and make it lock-correct: arm at rm_init_adapter API-lock acquisition (osapi.c:1761, in the kernel-open/unix layer where the flag is natively reachable) or at RmInitAdapter entry, so the flag is TRUE for the ENTIRE init window (pre-bootstrap AND poll). Then the foreground 200ms read is TRUE whenever the worker is anywhere in init -- which is exactly the wedge-prone window. Distinguish fast-fail from lockdown NOT by 'in the poll' but by the worker's COMPLETION: on fast-fail the worker has RETURNED by ~207ms (clearing the flag on its way out), so a flag still-TRUE at the foreground read means the worker is genuinely still stuck. This inverts the gate from 'in-poll' (racy, entered after 200ms) to 'still-in-init' (set early, cleared on worker return) and closes the timing race.

Equivalently/additionally adopt the bounded grace re-wait the design omitted (its 'closer (b)'): if the flag reads FALSE at the first 200ms check, re-wait a small bounded grace (e.g. 50-100ms) and re-read before deciding to skip C2. Given the lockdown poll-entry is just-past-200ms, a short grace would catch the TRUE transition; the fast-fail worker has already returned and cleared the flag, so the grace re-read stays FALSE for fast-fail. The design rejected (b) on the strength of the now-falsified deadlock proof -- with the proof broken, (b) (or the early-arm above) is REQUIRED, not optional.

Make the gate's SET point a worker-COMPLETION-aware signal rather than a poll-position signal. Concretely: set flag=TRUE at worker dispatch / init-entry, set flag=FALSE only when the worker RETURNS from nv_open_device_for_nvlfp (i.e. in nv_f40b_open_worker after the call, nv.c:1846, before complete()). Then 'flag TRUE at foreground 200ms read' == 'worker has not yet returned' == genuinely-still-running == apply C2. The fast-fail worker returns at ~207ms; if it returns before the foreground reads (race window ~2ms), flag is FALSE and C2 is skipped (self-heal preserved); if it has not returned, C2 is applied (harmless on fast-fail because the worker is about to return and C2 only marks a bus that the next open re-probes -- BUT this re-introduces the permanent-dead-bus regression on the ~2ms race, so this variant needs the substrate distinguished by the WPR2-result, not by timing). Prefer instead a flag that is set specifically at/after the WPR2-check PASS (kernel_gsp.c after 4768) so it is TRUE only on the lockdown substrate by construction and TRUE well before the poll -- this is the cleanest source-boundary-aligned arm point and avoids both the entry-too-late and the fast-fail-false-positive problems.

Resolve the C5-internal-os_pci_set_disconnected regression vector before claiming self-heal is restored: verify whether cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic (os.c:1921) sinks the bus on the transient fast-fail when the foreground's C5 rm_cleanup COND_ACQUIRE succeeds (worker already returned -> API lock free). If it does, gating only the nv.c:1914 C2 line is insufficient -- C5's sink must also be conditioned on the same lockdown-substrate signal, or rm_cleanup must be skipped entirely on the fast-fail timeout. Add an explicit test: WPR2-fast-fail n>=3, assert error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal AND subsequent open rc=0 with C5 reached.

Update the design's deadlock/locking model and the in-tree comments (nv.c:1902-1913, nv.c:1941-1956, nv.c:2322 A7) to reflect relaxed GSP init locking: state explicitly that during the gh100.c:1050 poll the worker holds the GPU GROUP lock, NOT the API lock (released at kernel_gsp.c:4785); that the API-lock contention the foreground sees is from the PRE-bootstrap RmInitAdapter phase; and that C1 (COND_ACQUIRE) plus cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic taking no GPU lock is what actually prevents the foreground from blocking on the worker. The current narrative ('worker holds the RM API write-lock across the poll') is the central factual error that produced the racy entry-arm decision.

Promote openQuestion-1 (NV_GET_NV_STATE reachability) to a hard requirement: implement the flag via an os-interface helper pair (os_tb_egpu_set/clear_in_lockdown_poll declared in both os-interface.h mirrors, implemented in the nv-tb-egpu-* surface), since the GSP TU cannot include unix nv.h. Keep the flag FIELD on nv_state_t after is_external_gpu (nv.h:556) per the gpu_lost_detector_logged precedent (nv.h:655, confirmed present), but reach it from deep-RM only through the helper, not NV_GET_NV_STATE.

critical: The design's entire race-safety argument ("RACE ARGUMENT (resolved)" + "WHY NO grace-rewait is needed") rests on a premise that is FALSE against source: it claims the worker provably HOLDS the RM API write-lock by the 200ms foreground read, inferred from the wedge having deadlocked in rm_cleanup's blocking API-lock acquire. But on a standard 5090 (Confidential Compute OFF -> SPDM disabled), relaxed GSP init locking is ENABLED BY DEFAULT: _kgspShouldRelaxGspInitLocking() returns NV_TRUE for the DEFAULT(0x2) regkey value (kernel_gsp.c:4694-4718, nvrm_registry.h:2820-2822), and _kgspBootGspRm() then calls rmapiLockRelease() at kernel_gsp.c:4783-4784 BEFORE kgspBootstrap_HAL at 4798. Therefore during the gpuTimeoutCondWait poll (gh100.c:1050) the worker holds ldata_lock + the GPU subdevice lock but NOT the API lock. SPDM is gated on CC (conf_compute.c:274; default NV_FALSE at g_spdm_nvoc.c:230), so the non-relaxed (API-lock-held-across-bootstrap) path is NOT the 5090 eGPU path. This means: (a) the design's deadlock-proof chain ("foreground deadlocked on the API lock the worker held -> worker was past entry 941 by 200ms") does not hold as stated, so the claim that no residual race remains is UNPROVEN by the cited evidence; and (b) it calls into question the wedge RCA's own mechanism ("worker holds the RM API lock across the multi-second poll") which the design inherits verbatim. The fix's FLAG mechanism itself is lock-independent and still works, but the justification for omitting the grace-rewait race-closer is invalid. Must re-derive what the worker actually holds at 200ms, re-establish (or refute) entry-before-200ms by other evidence, and reconsider whether the bounded grace-rewait closer is needed. This is the crux question the task explicitly asked to resolve with evidence, and the design resolved it on a false premise.
concerns: Clear-on-all-pre-poll-exits is under-specified for the actual code. The pre-poll bootstrap failures in kgspBootstrap_GH100 (kfspSendBootCommands @971, target-mask @999/1014, SPDM @1028/1029, initRPCs @1044) are MACRO returns (NV_CHECK_OK_OR_RETURN / NV_ASSERT_OK_OR_RETURN / NV_ASSERT_OR_RETURN, confirmed expand to `return` at nvassert.h:387/556/846), not plain `return status` lines. A "goto out_clear epilogue" does NOT intercept a macro that returns directly; each macro call site would have to be unwrapped or wrapped. The design flags the requirement correctly but its suggested implementations (single goto epilogue) won't catch the macro returns as written. If the entry-armed flag is left TRUE on a recoverable pre-poll failure, C2 would wrongly fire and permanently dead-bus a recoverable chip on the next timeout read — re-creating a regression of the same class v2 is meant to fix.

The ~4000ms lockdown-poll figure is a source-derived estimate (GPU_TIMEOUT_DEFAULT for gpuTimeoutCondWait), NOT a measured value — the lockdown substrate wedges the host before completion, so no log measures it to term. The forensic logs only directly evidence the 200ms budget and the ~205-210ms fast-fail. The design states ~4000ms as if forensically grounded; it should be labeled as the source-bound estimate it is. (Does not affect correctness of the fix, but affects the timing-claim provenance the task asked to cross-check.)

Design says insert the entry-arm at "gh100.c:941 (top of kgspBootstrap_GH100 body, pGpu in scope as first formal)". Line 941 is the opening brace; the first executable statement is line 949 (after local declarations at 942-946). The insert must go after the declarations, not literally at 941. Also pGpu is the FIRST formal (line 937) — correct — but the helper-based approach (preferred per open-question #1) sidesteps needing NV_GET_NV_STATE in this TU anyway.

The A7 shutdown-path C2 (nv.c:2322) is kept unconditional, which the design justifies as correct because the flag reads FALSE there. That is consistent with the entry-arm being in kgspBootstrap (open path) only. But note the A7 shutdown worker's in-tree comment (nv.c:2316-2321) ALSO claims the shutdown worker "can also stall in a GSP poll holding the RM API lock" — same stale API-lock premise as the open path. If the API-lock premise is wrong on the open path under relaxed locking, the shutdown-path comment is likely also imprecise. Not a correctness bug for v2 (A7 stays unconditional regardless), but the rationale text inherits the same flawed lock model and should be reconciled.

The fix correctly suppresses C2 on the fast-fail, but C5 rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state (nv.c:1919) stays OUTSIDE the gate and still runs on every fast-fail timeout. With C1 COND_ACQUIRE confirmed present (osapi.c:1913), on the fast-fail the worker has already returned so the API lock is free -> COND_ACQUIRE SUCCEEDS -> cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic runs -> it calls gpuSetDisconnectedProperties AND os_pci_set_disconnected (os.c:1938-1942) ITSELF. So C5, when its lock acquire succeeds on the fast-fail path, sets the SAME permanent dead-bus marker the gated C2 was trying to avoid. The design's claim that with C2 gated off "error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal" on fast-fail is only true if cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic does NOT run — but on fast-fail the lock is free so it likely DOES run. This must be checked: does C5/cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic also need gating (or does PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST not get set on a merely-fast-failed-but-alive chip)? If C5 sets the sink on fast-fail, the regression is NOT fixed by gating C2 alone.
suggestedChanges: Re-derive the worker's actual lock state at the 200ms read under relaxed GSP init locking (API lock RELEASED before bootstrap per kernel_gsp.c:4783-4784). Then either (a) find independent evidence that the worker reaches kgspBootstrap_GH100 entry (gh100.c:935) before 200ms on the lockdown substrate — e.g. instrument entry with an fsync'd marker in a contained repro — or (b) ADD the bounded grace-rewait race-closer the design currently omits, since the deadlock-proof justification for omitting it is invalid. Do not ship the omission on the current (false) proof.

CRITICAL-adjacent: verify whether C5 rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state (left outside the gate at nv.c:1919) sets the permanent dead-bus marker on the fast-fail path. With C1 COND_ACQUIRE and the worker already returned, the lock is free, so cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic (os.c:1921) runs and itself calls os_pci_set_disconnected (os.c:1942). If PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST can be set on the fast-failed chip, gating only C2 does NOT restore self-heal. Determine whether C5 must also be gated on the same flag, or prove cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic is a no-op on the fast-fail substrate (e.g. PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST not set there).

Implement the pre-poll-exit flag clearing as a genuine epilogue: convert the macro early-returns between gh100.c:949 and 1050 to `goto out_clear` form (unwrapping NV_CHECK_OK_OR_RETURN etc. at those sites), or arm the flag LATER than bootstrap entry but still provably before the poll and after the last pre-poll failure point — but only if the race re-derivation (suggested change 1) supports a later arm point. Add a build-time assert/test that the flag is FALSE after a simulated target-mask-release failure.

Prefer the os-interface helper-pair (os_tb_egpu_set/clear_in_lockdown_poll declared at os-interface.h:116-117 in BOTH mirrors, implemented in a TU that includes unix nv.h) over pulling unix nv.h into kernel_gsp_gh100.c — confirmed NV_GET_NV_STATE (nv.h:862) is NOT reachable in that TU (it includes only os/os.h, not unix nv.h). This keeps the deep-RM edit to one helper call at entry + one at poll-exit and stays sovereignty-minimal.

Update the stale in-tree comment at nv.c:1941-1956 (it asserts C5's acquire is BLOCKING and re-couples for up to gpuTimeout) since C1 COND_ACQUIRE at osapi.c:1913 is confirmed present on a10 HEAD d2a4e514. Also reconcile the A7 shutdown comment (nv.c:2316-2321) and the wedge RCA's "worker holds the RM API lock across the poll" statement against the relaxed-locking finding (API lock released before bootstrap). Re-label the ~4000ms lockdown figure as a source-derived gpuTimeout estimate, not a measured value.

Add the flag field as NvU32 after is_external_gpu in BOTH nv.h mirrors (kernel-open/common/inc/nv.h:556 and src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/include/nv.h:556 — confirmed identical) following the gpu_lost_detector_logged precedent (nv.h:655, written from os.c). Keep the defensive WRITE_ONCE(false) before queue_work (nv.c:1884) as designed.

critical: RACE ARGUMENT IS INVALID AS WRITTEN — the deadlock-proof does NOT establish the worker reached gh100.c:941. The RM API write-lock is acquired at osapi.c:1761 (rmapiLockAcquire NONE, immediately before RmInitAdapter at 1772), which is FAR UPSTREAM of kgspBootstrap_GH100 entry (gh100.c:941). The design asserts 'the lock is acquired AFTER entry 941' and 'forensics prove the worker HELD the API write-lock by 200ms therefore it is provably past 941' — both false. Holding the lock at 200ms only proves the worker is somewhere inside RmInitAdapter (could still be in kgspPopulateWprMeta / kgspPrepareForBootstrap / FSP send-boot-commands, all of which run while holding the lock and BEFORE the poll). With entry-arm at 941, a worker still in pre-941 init at the 200ms read leaves flag=FALSE -> C2 suppressed on a genuine lockdown stall. The 'no residual race remains' conclusion and the omission of the grace-rewait rest on this broken proof. CONSEQUENCE IS BOUNDED (not a re-wedge — see below) but the justification must be corrected and the race-closer reinstated or the arm-point moved.

RELAXED-LOCKING CONTRADICTS THE DEADLOCK RCA AND MUST BE RECONCILED BEFORE TRUSTING THE MECHANISM PREMISE. _kgspShouldRelaxGspInitLocking (kernel_gsp.c:4689) returns NV_TRUE by DEFAULT on Unix (regkey default 0x2 -> NV_TRUE at 4712-4715) UNLESS SPDM is enabled; SPDM is gated on Confidential Compute (conf_compute.c), which is OFF by default on a consumer RTX 5090. When relaxed locking is on, _kgspBootGspRm RELEASES the RM API lock at kernel_gsp.c:4785 BEFORE kgspBootstrap_HAL (4797) and re-acquires after (4818). That means during the lockdown poll the worker does NOT hold the API write-lock — directly contradicting the design's and the forensics doc's central premise ('worker holds the RM API write-lock across the multi-second poll'). Either SPDM/CC is actually enabled on this host (determine it), or the observed F44 deadlock arose from the GPU-lock / the narrow re-acquire window, not the API lock across the poll. Until this is settled, both the deadlock RCA and the entry-vs-poll race timing are built on an unverified premise. NOTE the fix's CONTAINMENT still works regardless (C2's os_pci_set_disconnected short-circuits osDevReadReg032 at os.c:2050 via osIsGpuBusDead/os_pci_is_disconnected, which is lock-free and independent of the API lock), but the design presents the lock-held premise as established fact and it is not.

DEEP-RM EARLY-EXIT CLEAR SITES ARE UNDERCOUNTED — there are 8 early-return sites between gh100.c:941 and the poll at 1050, not the ~5 the design lists (971/999/1014/1029/1044). The design omits the SEC2-path return (976/1006-1014), the _kgspBootstrapGspFmc_GH100 assert-return (980), and the SPDM rmapiLockIsOwner assert (1028). All are macro-hidden (NV_CHECK_OK_OR_RETURN / NV_ASSERT_OK_OR_RETURN / NV_ASSERT_OR_RETURN expand to `return rm_pvt_status`, confirmed nvassert.h:556/846/387). With entry-arm at 941, EVERY one of these must clear the flag or a pre-poll bootstrap failure (FSP/SEC2/target-mask/SPDM/initRPC — all NOT the lockdown substrate) leaves flag=TRUE and arms C2 on a RECOVERABLE failure. The design acknowledges clear-on-all-exits is REQUIRED but enumerates too few sites; a missed site is a silent C2-on-recoverable-failure regression. This ~10-edit deep-RM footprint (1 set + 8-10 clears + post-poll clear) is the heaviest part of the design and is avoidable (see suggestedChanges).
concerns: LOWER-BLAST-RADIUS PLACEMENT EXISTS AND SHOULD BE PREFERRED. Arming the flag at gh100.c:941 (bootstrap entry) forces clears at all 8+ early-return sites in arch-generic deep RM. Arming at POLL-ENTRY instead — one WRITE_ONCE(TRUE) immediately before gh100.c:1050 and one WRITE_ONCE(FALSE) immediately after — is just 2 deep-RM edits, needs NO early-return clears (the flag spans only the single gpuTimeoutCondWait call), and is structurally clean. The design rejected poll-entry on the stated race concern, but (a) that race concern rests on the invalid proof above, and (b) a false-negative is bounded to a soft wait, not a wedge. Poll-entry + a bounded grace-rewait closer is both lower-blast-radius AND race-safe.

FALSE-NEGATIVE IS NOT A RE-WEDGE (this de-risks the whole design but the design never says so). If the flag is FALSE at the 200ms read while the worker is genuinely stuck, C2 is skipped, the foreground falls through to C5 rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state which — with C1 COND_ACQUIRE confirmed present at osapi.c:1913 — CANNOT block, then hits the unconditional flush_work at nv.c:1957 which BLOCKS until the worker leaves (soft, host-alive, up to gpuTimeout). C1 alone breaks the lock-inversion that caused F44, so a flag false-negative degrades to the soft gpuTimeout wait, NOT a hard wedge. This should be stated explicitly as the safety floor; it is the reason a residual race is tolerable and the reason the grace-rewait is a refinement not a correctness gate.

DEEP-RM TU CANNOT WRITE nv_state_t DIRECTLY — kernel_gsp_gh100.c includes os/os.h (the RM PascalCase abstraction, src/nvidia/inc/kernel/os/os.h) but NOT the unix nv.h and NOT os-interface.h (verified: zero os_*() calls and no nv_state_t/NV_GET_NV_STATE in the TU). So the design's primary finding ('insert NV_GET_NV_STATE(pGpu); WRITE_ONCE(nv->...) at gh100.c:941') will not compile without pulling unix nv.h into arch-generic RM (a portability/sovereignty regression — this TU builds on non-unix platforms). The design's openQuestion #1 already flags this; it must be PROMOTED to a decided design point: route through an os-interface helper. But note: only 7 RM TUs include os-interface.h today, and gh100.c is not one — adding it widens the include. Cleanest is to declare the set/clear as RM-abstraction osXxx functions in os/os.h (already included) implemented in the unix os.c layer, OR keep the flag entirely in unix os.c and have gh100.c call a single os-abstraction toggle.

VALIDATION ON THE LOCKDOWN SUBSTRATE IS NOT YET POSSIBLE ON-DEMAND. The forensics doc states the lockdown substrate is stochastic (only when cycle-1 clears WPR2) and that live re-runs are BLOCKED on this very fix (#292). The design's openQuestion #4 asks for 'lockdown substrate at least one fire' validation, but there is no deterministic trigger. fake-5090 F44 model (task #290, deferred) is the realistic gate for the lockdown-arm. Plan the validation around the fast-fail self-heal (deterministic, n>=3) as the blocking gate and treat the lockdown-arm as best-effort-until-fake-5090.

A7 SHUTDOWN DECISION IS SOUND but the supporting reason is slightly off. The design says 'tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll reads FALSE on the shutdown path anyway.' Correct — the shutdown worker runs rm_shutdown_adapter, never RmInitAdapter/kgspBootstrap, so the flag is structurally FALSE there. Keeping A7's C2 UNCONDITIONAL is the right call (gating it on a flag that is always FALSE there would suppress C2 on every genuine teardown hang). Confirmed against the in-tree A7 C2 at nv.c:~2322. No change needed, but the in-tree A7 comment ('No-op on the WPR2-fast-fail substrate (timeout-only)') is loose and could be tightened to 'shutdown substrate has no fast-fail twin' for accuracy.
suggestedChanges: REINSTATE A RACE-CLOSER. Either (preferred) arm at POLL-ENTRY (1 WRITE_ONCE TRUE before gh100.c:1050, 1 WRITE_ONCE FALSE after) PLUS a bounded grace-rewait in the nv.c timeout branch: on timeout, if flag is FALSE, do one short bounded wait_for_completion_timeout (e.g. 50ms) and re-read the flag before deciding C2 — this catches a poll entered at ~195-250ms without the 8-site clear burden; OR keep entry-arm at 941 but ONLY if every early-exit clear is proven exhaustive (all 8 sites, ideally via a single-exit goto epilogue). Do not ship the entry-arm with the 'no residual race' claim and no closer — that combination is the actual risk.

CONFINE THE DEEP-RM EDIT TO 2 TOGGLE CALLS VIA AN OS-ABSTRACTION HELPER. Declare e.g. `void osTbEgpuSetInLockdownPoll(OBJGPU*, NvBool)` in os/os.h (already included by gh100.c), implement it in the unix os.c layer where NV_GET_NV_STATE + nv_state_t are already in scope (precedent: osIsGpuBusDead at os.c:1884 writes/reads nv via NV_GET_NV_STATE), have it WRITE_ONCE the nv_state_t flag. gh100.c then makes exactly 2 calls (poll-entry set / poll-exit clear) and needs NO new include and NO nv_state_t visibility. This keeps deep RM at 2 lines, is non-unix-safe (the helper is a no-op stub on non-unix), and matches the existing os-abstraction pattern. This is strictly lower blast radius than both the direct-nv_state_t-write and the os-interface.h os_*() helper variants in the design.

FIX THE STALE nv.c COMMENT in the SAME v2 commit. The in-tree nv.c:1941-1956 R0 comment claims C5 rm_cleanup takes a BLOCKING acquire and re-couples for up to gpuTimeout. C1 (COND_ACQUIRE) is CONFIRMED present at osapi.c:1913 on a10 HEAD d2a4e514, so that comment is STALE — C5 cannot block. Correct it to reflect COND_ACQUIRE; the worst-case latency on a flag false-negative is the flush_work join (worker leaving the poll), not a C5 block. The design's openQuestion #3 is hereby RESOLVED: COND_ACQUIRE is present.

INTEGRATION STEPS (do not execute): (1) On fork a10-f40b-lockfree-sink, stack a NEW v2 commit on d2a4e514 (do NOT amend) — touches: kernel-open/nvidia/nv.c (gate C2 at 1914), src/nvidia/src/kernel/gpu/gsp/arch/hopper/kernel_gsp_gh100.c (2 toggle calls), src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/src/os.c (helper impl), src/nvidia/inc/kernel/os/os.h (helper decl), BOTH nv.h mirrors (kernel-open/common/inc/nv.h:556-ish AND src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/include/nv.h same line — they are kept field-in-lockstep though the files differ overall; add the field adjacent to is_external_gpu), nv.c:1941-1956 comment fix, and version.mk already at 595.71.05-apnex.26. (2) Add manifest row `A10-f40b-lockfree-sink addon - fork:a10-f40b-lockfree-sink` AFTER the A9 row in /root/nvidia-driver-injector/patches/manifest (currently ends at A9; there is NO A10 row or patch file yet — A10 has never been regen'd). (3) Run tools/regen-base-patches.sh (FORK_REPO=/root/open-gpu-kernel-modules) to emit patches/addon/A10-f40b-lockfree-sink.patch; verify a10 still descends from tag 595.71.05 (it does). (4) Author docs/patch-intents/A10-f40b-lockfree-sink.md from _template.md (none exists yet) documenting both C2-contain (v1) and the flag-gate de-regression (v2), the relaxed-locking caveat, and the fast-fail vs lockdown substrate split. (5) Container rebuild: Dockerfile NVIDIA_OPEN_TAG=595.71.05 and NVIDIA_NVID_VERSION=595.71.05 are UNCHANGED, so the /lib/firmware/nvidia/595.71.05 path is unchanged — the task #294 firmware-symlink gotcha does NOT bite this bump (no full-version change), but still verify the symlink resolves post-build before reboot per the 2026-05-22 lesson encoded at Dockerfile:91-99. (6) Validation gate: deterministic fast-fail self-heal n>=3 (flag FALSE, error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal, subsequent open rc=0) is the BLOCKING gate; lockdown-arm fire is best-effort-until-fake-5090 (task #290).

ADD a tiny defensive WRITE_ONCE(nv->...lockdown_poll, NV_FALSE) before queue_work at nv.c:1884 (design already proposes this) — it is single-threaded at that point (pre-queue) so race-free, and it prevents a prior open's residual TRUE leaking into a later fast-fail open's timeout read. Keep it; it is cheap insurance and orthogonal to the arm-point choice.

final.summary: A10-v2: relaxed GSP locking falsifies the entry-arm proof; gate BOTH C2 and C5 on a worker flag plus a grace re-wait.
findings: Relaxed locking releases the API lock at kernel_gsp.c:4785 before the gh100.c:1050 poll
WPR2 fast-fail bails at 4768 at about 207ms, after the 200ms read
Flag tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll on nv_state_t at nv.h:556 in both mirrors
Helper in nv-gpu-lost.h impl in os.c, not NV_GET_NV_STATE in gh100.c
Arm after WPR2 return at 4771, clear at kgspInitRm done at 5128
Gate BOTH C2 nv.c:1914 and C5 nv.c:1919; DESIGN missed C5
C5 sinks the bus on fast-fail via os.c:1942 even with C2 gated
Add a 150ms grace re-wait then re-read the flag
A7 2322 stays unconditional; C1 flush_work 1957 A8 intact
openQuestions: Validate fast-fail self-heals rc 0 with no sink
